revision.text:
'''Saeki clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) refers to a group of families bearing the name "Saeki" as their [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] (clan name). __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == The Saeki clan traces its origins to {{ill|Amenootsuhiko|ja|еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenootsuhiko|qq=}} (Ame no Oshi-hi no Mikoto), who guided {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|еҪҰзҒ«з“ҠгҖ…жқөе°Ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} during the [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the heavenly grandson]]. The clan split from the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|ЕҢtomo clan]] under the leadership of {{ill|ЕҢtomo no Muroya|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo no Muroya|qq=}}, becoming a {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} (descendant of deities) lineage of {{ill|Tenshin|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin|qq=}} origin. The clan name "Saeki" is believed to derive from their role in guarding the imperial court, as they were seen as those who "block (йҒ® sahe) external threats." This evolved into "Saeki" (гҒ•гҒҲгҒҚ) in modern phonetics, and in some cases as "Saiki" (гҒ•гӮҗгҒҚ). The Saeki clan served as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=}}, leading the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki-be]], who were tasked with guarding the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court of Japan]]. The gates they protected were named "Saeki Gates," though during the Heian period, under {{ill|Tang culture|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang culture|qq=}} influences, the name was changed to "Mokabe Gate" (и—»еЈҒй–Җ). Initially granted the {{ill|Kabane|ja|е§“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of "Muraji" (йҖЈ), the Saeki clan, alongside the ЕҢtomo clan and others, was elevated to the Rank of "Sukune" (е®ҝзҰ°) during the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685 CE). Prominent figures in the Saeki clan included {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|qq=}}, who assisted in the assassination of Soga no Iruka, and {{ill|Saeki no Imaemaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imaemaro|qq=}}, a statesman who served as a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. Military leaders such as {{ill|Saeki no Tsurugite|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Tsurugite|qq=}}, who served under Soga no Umako, and {{ill|Saeki no Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ishiyu|qq=}}, a general during campaigns against the Emishi, were also notable. However, by the end of the Heian period, the clan gradually declined from mid-level aristocracy to {{ill|Chikage|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikage|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}} (bureaucrats of lower Rank). The Saeki name was carried on by families like the Ono family, who produced the herbalist [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ranzan]], and artists such as [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Ganku]] of the Kishi school. == Related Lineages == === Saeki no Miyatsuko (дҪҗдјҜйҖ ) The Saeki no Miyatsuko lineage is documented in the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], appearing under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Shinbetsu. They were originally known as "Saeki-be" and managed the Saeki-be labor group. === Saeki no Obito (дҪҗдјҜйҰ–) The Saeki no Obito line appears in the Shinbetsu of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] as recorded in the Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku. == Saeki-be == The Saeki-be, managed by the central Saeki clan, was a labor group organized as part of the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Shinbetsu]] system. Local Saeki-be groups were supervised by the {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} lineage. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|author=Yuki Saeki|authorlink=дҪҗдјҜжңүжё…|chapter=е®®еҹҺеҚҒдәҢй–ҖиҷҹгҒЁеҸӨд»ЈеӨ©зҡҮиҝ‘дҫҚж°Ҹж—Ҹ|date=1963-4|volume=з ”з©¶зҜҮ|title=ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІгҒ®з ”з©¶|publisher=еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|isbn=464202pp08|ref={{SfnRef|дҪҗдјҜ|1963}}}} * {{Citation|е’Ңжӣё|last=Saeki|first=Yuki|year=1982|date=1982-7|volume=иҖғиӯүзҜҮ з¬¬3|title=ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІгҒ®з ”з©¶|publisher=еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|isbn=464202pp32|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reform]] * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] * {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|ж©ҳеҘҲиүҜйә»е‘ӮгҒ®д№ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} * [[:en:Mount Tate|Tateyama, Toyama]] * [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|Oyama Shrine]] * {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{translated page|ja|дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ|version=102894177|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Saeki Clan]][[sn:Saeki Clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Saeki Clan]] }} [[sn:Saeki Clan]] [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] ==Merged second translation== {{Missing footnotes|date=December 2018}} {{Other uses list|Saeki Clan of the Central Clan|Saeki Clan (ЕҢgami) of Bungo Province|Saeki Clan (Bungo Province)|Saeki Clan of Aki Province|Itsukushima Shinto Priest Family|Saeki Clan of Regional Aristocrats|Saeki Nao|}} {{Japanese clans (Ancient clans) |Clan Name = Saeki Clan |image = |Clan Surname = Saeki [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]] |Ancestor = |Origin = |Clan Founder = {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} |Type = {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}пјҲ{{ill|Heavenly Deity|de|Himmlische Gottheit|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|lt=Heavenly Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©зҘһ|lt_de=Himmlische Gottheit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Deity|qq=}}пјү |Main Lineage = |Notable Figure = {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}} |Descendants = {{ill|Saeki Tsukuri|de|Saeki Tsukuri|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tsukuri|qq=}}
{{ill|Saeki Obito|de|Saeki Obito|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҰ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Obito|qq=}} }} '''Saeki Clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) is a [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] whose name is "Saeki". __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == During the time of [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the Heavenly Grandson]], the ancestor, [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Hikohirakininomikoto]], who was led by {{ill|Amenooshihi|de|Amenooshihi|ja|гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|lt=Heavenly Push Day Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|lt_de=Amenooshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi|qq=}}, (Amenooshihi no Mikoto), is said to be the progenitor, and it is a {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan that split from the Otomo clan during the time of {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ [[:en:Sakyo|Sakyo]], in the Divine Separation section.. After serving the royal authority and undertaking duties such as palace security, there is a theory that they came to be called "Saeki", a term meaning "those who block" (йҒ®гҒҺгӮӢиҖ…), referring to repelling external enemy attacks ([[:en:Historical kana orthography|Historical Kana Usage]] spells it "saheki", while [[:en:Modern kana usage|Modern Kana Usage]] spells it "saeki"). There is also a dialectal pronunciation "saiki" (also "sawiki"). As the central {{ill|BanzЕҚ|de|BanzЕҚ|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/BanzЕҚ|qq=}}, they led the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]], serving the {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|жңқе»· (ж—Ҙжң¬)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja=жңқе»·|lt_de=Kaiserhof|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|qq=}} as palace gate guards and as a military force. Incidentally, the palace gate in charge of security was named {{ill|Saeki Gate|de|Saeki-Tor|ja|дҪҗдјҜй–Җ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Gate|qq=}} after the clan name, but at the {{ill|Heian Palace|de|Heian-Palast|ja|е№іе®үе®®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Palace|qq=}}, under the influence of {{ill|Tang-style Culture|de|Tang-Kultur|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-style Culture|qq=}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Moss Wall Gate|de|Mooswandtor|ja|и—»еЈҒй–Җ|lt=Moss Wall Gate (SЕҚheki Gate)|lt_ja=и—»еЈҒпјҲгҒқгҒҶгҒёгҒҚпјүй–Җ|lt_de=Mooswandtor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moss Wall Gate|qq=}}вҖҳ[[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiriyaku]]вҖҷ [[:en:KЕҚnin (era)|KЕҚnin]] 9th year ([[:en:818|818 AD]]) April 27 clause.. The [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] was originally [[:en:Muraji|Ren]], but in the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] ([[:en:685|685 AD]]), together with members of the same group such as the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], they were granted the surname [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]]. In the {{ill|Main Palace Office|de|Hauptpalastamt|ja|дё»ж®ҝеҜ®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace Office|qq=}}, the name of the Saeki faction remains as one of the noble divisions. Notable figures include {{ill|Saeki Komaro|de|Saeki Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Komaro|qq=}}, who, following {{ill|Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|de|Prinz Nakano-ЕҢe|ja|дёӯеӨ§е…„зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|qq=}}, assassinated [[:en:Soga no Iruka|Soga no Iruka]], and {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}}, who was active in government from the Nara to the Heian period and held positions such as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Mikoto|de|Dazai no Mikoto|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Mikoto|qq=}}. The clan also produced many warriors, including {{ill|Saeki Tankeide|de|Saeki Tankeide|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tankeide|qq=}} (the father of Komaro), who, following [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], executed {{ill|Prince Anahobe|de|Prinz Anahobe|ja|з©ҙз©ӮйғЁзҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anahobe|qq=}}; {{ill|Saeki Ishiyu|de|Saeki Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ishiyu|qq=}}, the Ezo General after the conquest of Echigo; and {{ill|Saeki Katsuragi|de|Saeki Katsuragi|ja|дҪҗдјҜи‘ӣеҹҺ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Katsuragi|qq=}} (the grandson of Ishiyu), the Deputy General of the Eastern Expedition. Apart from Imao Hito, no one ascended to the position of {{ill|Giseikan|de|Giseikan|ja|иӯ°ж”ҝе®ҳ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giseikan|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}, and the clan eventually declined from being mid-level nobles to lower-Ranked officials ({{ill|Underground Officials|de|Underground Officials|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|lt=Underground Officials|lt_ja=ең°дёӢе®ҳдәә|lt_de=Underground Officials|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}}). Until around the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku Period]], together with the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], {{ill|Wake Clan|de|Wake Clan|ja|е’Ңж°—ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Clan|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi clan|Baekje Royal Family]], the clan was considered one that received {{ill|Noble Titles|de|Adelstitel|ja|ж°ҸзҲө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noble Titles|qq=}} upon the accession of an emperor{{ill|Tajima no Kimoto|de|Tajima no Kimoto|ja|з”°еі¶е…¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima no Kimoto|qq=}} "The Establishment of Noble Titles вҖ“ Rituals, Service, and Conferment" in {{sfn|еҸІжһ—}} 71-1, [[:en:1988|1988 AD]], p. 61. Among the lower-Ranked officials bearing the Saeki name, there is the {{ill|Ono Family|de|Ono-Familie|ja|е°ҸйҮҺе®¶ (дҪҗдјҜе§“)|lt=Ono Family|lt_ja=е°ҸйҮҺе®¶|lt_de=Ono-Familie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family|qq=}}, which produced the materia medica scholar [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ransan]], and the painter [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Koma]], who founded the {{ill|Kishi School|de|Kishi-Schule|ja|еІёжҙҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi School|qq=}} and served the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]] family, bearing the Saeki name. == Related Clans == === Saeki Tsukuri === '''Saeki Tsukuri''' (-Miyatsuko) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Divine Separation section, and his former name was "Saeki Faction". According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he was a retainer of [[:en:Ichinobe no Oshiwa|Ichinobe Oshi]] and a descendant of {{ill|Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|de|Saeki Fraktion Naka-chiko|ja|дҪҗдјҜйғЁд»Іеӯҗ|lt=Naka-chiko|lt_ja=гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“|lt_de=Naka-chiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|qq=}}In the Pre-accession Annals of [[:en:Emperor YЕ«ryaku|Emperor YЕ«ryaku]], in the 3rd year, October 1 clause of [[:en:Emperor AnkЕҚ|Emperor Ankan]], he is referred to as "Saeki Faction Uruba" (вҖ“uruba), also known as "Naka-teko" (гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“). and, in the 5th year, February 5 clause of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], he was appointed to oversee the Saeki factions from various provinces. === Saeki Obito === '''Saeki Obito''' (-Obito) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] under the {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} category. == Saeki Faction == The Saeki Clan oversaw the central [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]] within the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Official Division]],{{Cite kotobank|Saeki Clan|encyclopedia=Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten 2nd edition|accessdate=2021-12-19}} while the regional Saeki Faction was overseen by {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki Nao|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Nao|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}. == Footnotes == '''Saeki clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) refers to a group of families bearing the name "Saeki" as their [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] (clan name). __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == The Saeki clan traces its origins to {{ill|Amenootsuhiko|ja|еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenootsuhiko|qq=}} (Ame no Oshi-hi no Mikoto), who guided {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|еҪҰзҒ«з“ҠгҖ…жқөе°Ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} during the [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the heavenly grandson]]. The clan split from the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|ЕҢtomo clan]] under the leadership of {{ill|ЕҢtomo no Muroya|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo no Muroya|qq=}}, becoming a {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} (descendant of deities) lineage of {{ill|Tenshin|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin|qq=}} origin. The clan name "Saeki" is believed to derive from their role in guarding the imperial court, as they were seen as those who "block (йҒ® sahe) external threats." This evolved into "Saeki" (гҒ•гҒҲгҒҚ) in modern phonetics, and in some cases as "Saiki" (гҒ•гӮҗгҒҚ). The Saeki clan served as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=}}, leading the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki-be]], who were tasked with guarding the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court of Japan]]. The gates they protected were named "Saeki Gates," though during the Heian period, under {{ill|Tang culture|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang culture|qq=}} influences, the name was changed to "Mokabe Gate" (и—»еЈҒй–Җ). Initially granted the {{ill|Kabane|ja|е§“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of "Muraji" (йҖЈ), the Saeki clan, alongside the ЕҢtomo clan and others, was elevated to the Rank of "Sukune" (е®ҝзҰ°) during the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685 CE). Prominent figures in the Saeki clan included {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|qq=}}, who assisted in the assassination of Soga no Iruka, and {{ill|Saeki no Imaemaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imaemaro|qq=}}, a statesman who served as a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. Military leaders such as {{ill|Saeki no Tsurugite|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Tsurugite|qq=}}, who served under Soga no Umako, and {{ill|Saeki no Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ishiyu|qq=}}, a general during campaigns against the Emishi, were also notable. However, by the end of the Heian period, the clan gradually declined from mid-level aristocracy to {{ill|Chikage|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikage|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}} (bureaucrats of lower Rank). The Saeki name was carried on by families like the Ono family, who produced the herbalist [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ranzan]], and artists such as [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Ganku]] of the Kishi school. == Related Lineages == === Saeki no Miyatsuko (дҪҗдјҜйҖ ) The Saeki no Miyatsuko lineage is documented in the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], appearing under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Shinbetsu. They were originally known as "Saeki-be" and managed the Saeki-be labor group. === Saeki no Obito (дҪҗдјҜйҰ–) The Saeki no Obito line appears in the Shinbetsu of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] as recorded in the Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku. == Saeki-be == The Saeki-be, managed by the central Saeki clan, was a labor group organized as part of the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Shinbetsu]] system. Local Saeki-be groups were supervised by the {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} lineage. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite |Notable Figure = {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}} |Descendants = {{ill|Saeki Tsukuri|de|Saeki Tsukuri|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tsukuri|qq=}}
{{ill|Saeki Obito|de|Saeki Obito|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҰ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Obito|qq=}} }} '''Saeki Clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) is a [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] whose name is "Saeki". __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == During the time of [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the Heavenly Grandson]], the ancestor, [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Hikohirakininomikoto]], who was led by {{ill|Amenooshihi|de|Amenooshihi|ja|гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|lt=Heavenly Push Day Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|lt_de=Amenooshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi|qq=}}, (Amenooshihi no Mikoto), is said to be the progenitor, and it is a {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan that split from the Otomo clan during the time of {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ [[:en:Sakyo|Sakyo]], in the Divine Separation section.. After serving the royal authority and undertaking duties such as palace security, there is a theory that they came to be called "Saeki", a term meaning "those who block" (йҒ®гҒҺгӮӢиҖ…), referring to repelling external enemy attacks ([[:en:Historical kana orthography|Historical Kana Usage]] spells it "saheki", while [[:en:Modern kana usage|Modern Kana Usage]] spells it "saeki"). There is also a dialectal pronunciation "saiki" (also "sawiki"). As the central {{ill|BanzЕҚ|de|BanzЕҚ|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/BanzЕҚ|qq=}}, they led the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]], serving the {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|жңқе»· (ж—Ҙжң¬)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja=жңқе»·|lt_de=Kaiserhof|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|qq=}} as palace gate guards and as a military force. Incidentally, the palace gate in charge of security was named {{ill|Saeki Gate|de|Saeki-Tor|ja|дҪҗдјҜй–Җ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Gate|qq=}} after the clan name, but at the {{ill|Heian Palace|de|Heian-Palast|ja|е№іе®үе®®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Palace|qq=}}, under the influence of {{ill|Tang-style Culture|de|Tang-Kultur|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-style Culture|qq=}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Moss Wall Gate|de|Mooswandtor|ja|и—»еЈҒй–Җ|lt=Moss Wall Gate (SЕҚheki Gate)|lt_ja=и—»еЈҒпјҲгҒқгҒҶгҒёгҒҚпјүй–Җ|lt_de=Mooswandtor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moss Wall Gate|qq=}}вҖҳ[[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiriyaku]]вҖҷ [[:en:KЕҚnin (era)|KЕҚnin]] 9th year ([[:en:818|818 AD]]) April 27 clause.. The [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] was originally [[:en:Muraji|Ren]], but in the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] ([[:en:685|685 AD]]), together with members of the same group such as the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], they were granted the surname [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]]. In the {{ill|Main Palace Office|de|Hauptpalastamt|ja|дё»ж®ҝеҜ®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace Office|qq=}}, the name of the Saeki faction remains as one of the noble divisions. Notable figures include {{ill|Saeki Komaro|de|Saeki Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Komaro|qq=}}, who, following {{ill|Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|de|Prinz Nakano-ЕҢe|ja|дёӯеӨ§е…„зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|qq=}}, assassinated [[:en:Soga no Iruka|Soga no Iruka]], and {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}}, who was active in government from the Nara to the Heian period and held positions such as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Mikoto|de|Dazai no Mikoto|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Mikoto|qq=}}. The clan also produced many warriors, including {{ill|Saeki Tankeide|de|Saeki Tankeide|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tankeide|qq=}} (the father of Komaro), who, following [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], executed {{ill|Prince Anahobe|de|Prinz Anahobe|ja|з©ҙз©ӮйғЁзҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anahobe|qq=}}; {{ill|Saeki Ishiyu|de|Saeki Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ishiyu|qq=}}, the Ezo General after the conquest of Echigo; and {{ill|Saeki Katsuragi|de|Saeki Katsuragi|ja|дҪҗдјҜи‘ӣеҹҺ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Katsuragi|qq=}} (the grandson of Ishiyu), the Deputy General of the Eastern Expedition. Apart from Imao Hito, no one ascended to the position of {{ill|Giseikan|de|Giseikan|ja|иӯ°ж”ҝе®ҳ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giseikan|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}, and the clan eventually declined from being mid-level nobles to lower-Ranked officials ({{ill|Underground Officials|de|Underground Officials|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|lt=Underground Officials|lt_ja=ең°дёӢе®ҳдәә|lt_de=Underground Officials|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}}). Until around the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku Period]], together with the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], {{ill|Wake Clan|de|Wake Clan|ja|е’Ңж°—ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Clan|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi clan|Baekje Royal Family]], the clan was considered one that received {{ill|Noble Titles|de|Adelstitel|ja|ж°ҸзҲө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noble Titles|qq=}} upon the accession of an emperor{{ill|Tajima no Kimoto|de|Tajima no Kimoto|ja|з”°еі¶е…¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima no Kimoto|qq=}} "The Establishment of Noble Titles вҖ“ Rituals, Service, and Conferment" in {{sfn|еҸІжһ—}} 71-1, [[:en:1988|1988 AD]], p. 61. Among the lower-Ranked officials bearing the Saeki name, there is the {{ill|Ono Family|de|Ono-Familie|ja|е°ҸйҮҺе®¶ (дҪҗдјҜе§“)|lt=Ono Family|lt_ja=е°ҸйҮҺе®¶|lt_de=Ono-Familie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family|qq=}}, which produced the materia medica scholar [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ransan]], and the painter [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Koma]], who founded the {{ill|Kishi School|de|Kishi-Schule|ja|еІёжҙҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi School|qq=}} and served the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]] family, bearing the Saeki name. == Related Clans == === Saeki Tsukuri === '''Saeki Tsukuri''' (-Miyatsuko) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Divine Separation section, and his former name was "Saeki Faction". According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he was a retainer of [[:en:Ichinobe no Oshiwa|Ichinobe Oshi]] and a descendant of {{ill|Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|de|Saeki Fraktion Naka-chiko|ja|дҪҗдјҜйғЁд»Іеӯҗ|lt=Naka-chiko|lt_ja=гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“|lt_de=Naka-chiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|qq=}}In the Pre-accession Annals of [[:en:Emperor YЕ«ryaku|Emperor YЕ«ryaku]], in the 3rd year, October 1 clause of [[:en:Emperor AnkЕҚ|Emperor Ankan]], he is referred to as "Saeki Faction Uruba" (вҖ“uruba), also known as "Naka-teko" (гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“). and, in the 5th year, February 5 clause of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], he was appointed to oversee the Saeki factions from various provinces. === Saeki Obito === '''Saeki Obito''' (-Obito) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] under the {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} category. == Saeki Faction == The Saeki Clan oversaw the central [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]] within the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Official Division]],{{Cite kotobank|Saeki Clan|encyclopedia=Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten 2nd edition|accessdate=2021-12-19}} while the regional Saeki Faction was overseen by {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki Nao|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Nao|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Saeki YЕ«kД“|authorlink=Saeki YЕ«kД“|chapter=The Twelve Palace Gate Names of Miyagi and the Ancient Imperial Attendants Clan|date=April 1963|volume=Research Volume|edition=|title=Study of the New Compilation of Surnames|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|de|Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|ja|еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|qq=}}|isbn=4642021108|ref={{SfnRef|Saeki|1963|}}}} * {{Citation|language=Japanese|last=Saeki|first=YЕ«kД“|year=1982a|date=March 1982|volume=Textual Verification Volume Part 2|edition=|title=Study of the New Compilation of Surnames|publisher=Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|isbn=4642021124|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|language=Japanese|last=Saeki|first=YЕ«kД“|year=1982b|date=July 1982|volume=Textual Verification Volume Part 3|edition=|title=Study of the New Compilation of Surnames|publisher=Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|isbn=4642021132|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|language=Japanese|last=Saeki|first=YЕ«kД“|year=1982c|date=November 1982|volume=Textual Verification (Nipponica)|accessdate=2021-12-19|ref={{SfnRef|Nihon Dai Hyakka Jiten}}}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reform]] * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} * [[:en:Mount Tate|Mount Tate]] * [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|Oyama Shrine]] * {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki revision.text:
'''Saeki clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) refers to a group of families bearing the name "Saeki" as their [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] (clan name). __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == The Saeki clan traces its origins to {{ill|Amenootsuhiko|ja|еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenootsuhiko|qq=}} (Ame no Oshi-hi no Mikoto), who guided {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|еҪҰзҒ«з“ҠгҖ…жқөе°Ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} during the [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the heavenly grandson]]. The clan split from the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|ЕҢtomo clan]] under the leadership of {{ill|ЕҢtomo no Muroya|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo no Muroya|qq=}}, becoming a {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} (descendant of deities) lineage of {{ill|Tenshin|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin|qq=}} origin. The clan name "Saeki" is believed to derive from their role in guarding the imperial court, as they were seen as those who "block (йҒ® sahe) external threats." This evolved into "Saeki" (гҒ•гҒҲгҒҚ) in modern phonetics, and in some cases as "Saiki" (гҒ•гӮҗгҒҚ). The Saeki clan served as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=}}, leading the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki-be]], who were tasked with guarding the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court of Japan]]. The gates they protected were named "Saeki Gates," though during the Heian period, under {{ill|Tang culture|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang culture|qq=}} influences, the name was changed to "Mokabe Gate" (и—»еЈҒй–Җ). Initially granted the {{ill|Kabane|ja|е§“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of "Muraji" (йҖЈ), the Saeki clan, alongside the ЕҢtomo clan and others, was elevated to the Rank of "Sukune" (е®ҝзҰ°) during the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685 CE). Prominent figures in the Saeki clan included {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|qq=}}, who assisted in the assassination of Soga no Iruka, and {{ill|Saeki no Imaemaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imaemaro|qq=}}, a statesman who served as a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. Military leaders such as {{ill|Saeki no Tsurugite|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Tsurugite|qq=}}, who served under Soga no Umako, and {{ill|Saeki no Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ishiyu|qq=}}, a general during campaigns against the Emishi, were also notable. However, by the end of the Heian period, the clan gradually declined from mid-level aristocracy to {{ill|Chikage|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikage|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}} (bureaucrats of lower Rank). The Saeki name was carried on by families like the Ono family, who produced the herbalist [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ranzan]], and artists such as [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Ganku]] of the Kishi school. == Related Lineages == === Saeki no Miyatsuko (дҪҗдјҜйҖ ) The Saeki no Miyatsuko lineage is documented in the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], appearing under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Shinbetsu. They were originally known as "Saeki-be" and managed the Saeki-be labor group. === Saeki no Obito (дҪҗдјҜйҰ–) The Saeki no Obito line appears in the Shinbetsu of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] as recorded in the Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku. == Saeki-be == The Saeki-be, managed by the central Saeki clan, was a labor group organized as part of the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Shinbetsu]] system. Local Saeki-be groups were supervised by the {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} lineage. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|author=Yuki Saeki|authorlink=дҪҗдјҜжңүжё…|chapter=е®®еҹҺеҚҒдәҢй–ҖиҷҹгҒЁеҸӨд»ЈеӨ©зҡҮиҝ‘дҫҚж°Ҹж—Ҹ|date=1963-4|volume=з ”з©¶зҜҮ|title=ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІгҒ®з ”з©¶|publisher=еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|isbn=464202pp08|ref={{SfnRef|дҪҗдјҜ|1963}}}} * {{Citation|е’Ңжӣё|last=Saeki|first=Yuki|year=1982|date=1982-7|volume=иҖғиӯүзҜҮ з¬¬3|title=ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІгҒ®з ”з©¶|publisher=еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|isbn=464202pp32|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reform]] * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] * {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|ж©ҳеҘҲиүҜйә»е‘ӮгҒ®д№ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} * [[:en:Mount Tate|Tateyama, Toyama]] * [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|Oyama Shrine]] * {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{translated page|ja|дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ|version=102894177|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Saeki Clan]][[sn:Saeki Clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Saeki Clan]] }} [[sn:Saeki Clan]] [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] ==Merged second translation== {{Missing footnotes|date=December 2018}} {{Other uses list|Saeki Clan of the Central Clan|Saeki Clan (ЕҢgami) of Bungo Province|Saeki Clan (Bungo Province)|Saeki Clan of Aki Province|Itsukushima Shinto Priest Family|Saeki Clan of Regional Aristocrats|Saeki Nao|}} {{Japanese clans (Ancient clans) |Clan Name = Saeki Clan |image = |Clan Surname = Saeki [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]] |Ancestor = |Origin = |Clan Founder = {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} |Type = {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}пјҲ{{ill|Heavenly Deity|de|Himmlische Gottheit|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|lt=Heavenly Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©зҘһ|lt_de=Himmlische Gottheit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Deity|qq=}}пјү |Main Lineage = |Notable Figure = {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}} |Descendants = {{ill|Saeki Tsukuri|de|Saeki Tsukuri|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tsukuri|qq=}}
{{ill|Saeki Obito|de|Saeki Obito|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҰ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Obito|qq=}} }} '''Saeki Clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) is a [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] whose name is "Saeki". __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == During the time of [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the Heavenly Grandson]], the ancestor, [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Hikohirakininomikoto]], who was led by {{ill|Amenooshihi|de|Amenooshihi|ja|гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|lt=Heavenly Push Day Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|lt_de=Amenooshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi|qq=}}, (Amenooshihi no Mikoto), is said to be the progenitor, and it is a {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan that split from the Otomo clan during the time of {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ [[:en:Sakyo|Sakyo]], in the Divine Separation section.. After serving the royal authority and undertaking duties such as palace security, there is a theory that they came to be called "Saeki", a term meaning "those who block" (йҒ®гҒҺгӮӢиҖ…), referring to repelling external enemy attacks ([[:en:Historical kana orthography|Historical Kana Usage]] spells it "saheki", while [[:en:Modern kana usage|Modern Kana Usage]] spells it "saeki"). There is also a dialectal pronunciation "saiki" (also "sawiki"). As the central {{ill|BanzЕҚ|de|BanzЕҚ|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/BanzЕҚ|qq=}}, they led the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]], serving the {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|жңқе»· (ж—Ҙжң¬)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja=жңқе»·|lt_de=Kaiserhof|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|qq=}} as palace gate guards and as a military force. Incidentally, the palace gate in charge of security was named {{ill|Saeki Gate|de|Saeki-Tor|ja|дҪҗдјҜй–Җ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Gate|qq=}} after the clan name, but at the {{ill|Heian Palace|de|Heian-Palast|ja|е№іе®үе®®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Palace|qq=}}, under the influence of {{ill|Tang-style Culture|de|Tang-Kultur|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-style Culture|qq=}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Moss Wall Gate|de|Mooswandtor|ja|и—»еЈҒй–Җ|lt=Moss Wall Gate (SЕҚheki Gate)|lt_ja=и—»еЈҒпјҲгҒқгҒҶгҒёгҒҚпјүй–Җ|lt_de=Mooswandtor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moss Wall Gate|qq=}}вҖҳ[[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiriyaku]]вҖҷ [[:en:KЕҚnin (era)|KЕҚnin]] 9th year ([[:en:818|818 AD]]) April 27 clause.. The [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] was originally [[:en:Muraji|Ren]], but in the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] ([[:en:685|685 AD]]), together with members of the same group such as the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], they were granted the surname [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]]. In the {{ill|Main Palace Office|de|Hauptpalastamt|ja|дё»ж®ҝеҜ®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace Office|qq=}}, the name of the Saeki faction remains as one of the noble divisions. Notable figures include {{ill|Saeki Komaro|de|Saeki Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Komaro|qq=}}, who, following {{ill|Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|de|Prinz Nakano-ЕҢe|ja|дёӯеӨ§е…„зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|qq=}}, assassinated [[:en:Soga no Iruka|Soga no Iruka]], and {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}}, who was active in government from the Nara to the Heian period and held positions such as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Mikoto|de|Dazai no Mikoto|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Mikoto|qq=}}. The clan also produced many warriors, including {{ill|Saeki Tankeide|de|Saeki Tankeide|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tankeide|qq=}} (the father of Komaro), who, following [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], executed {{ill|Prince Anahobe|de|Prinz Anahobe|ja|з©ҙз©ӮйғЁзҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anahobe|qq=}}; {{ill|Saeki Ishiyu|de|Saeki Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ishiyu|qq=}}, the Ezo General after the conquest of Echigo; and {{ill|Saeki Katsuragi|de|Saeki Katsuragi|ja|дҪҗдјҜи‘ӣеҹҺ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Katsuragi|qq=}} (the grandson of Ishiyu), the Deputy General of the Eastern Expedition. Apart from Imao Hito, no one ascended to the position of {{ill|Giseikan|de|Giseikan|ja|иӯ°ж”ҝе®ҳ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giseikan|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}, and the clan eventually declined from being mid-level nobles to lower-Ranked officials ({{ill|Underground Officials|de|Underground Officials|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|lt=Underground Officials|lt_ja=ең°дёӢе®ҳдәә|lt_de=Underground Officials|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}}). Until around the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku Period]], together with the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], {{ill|Wake Clan|de|Wake Clan|ja|е’Ңж°—ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Clan|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi clan|Baekje Royal Family]], the clan was considered one that received {{ill|Noble Titles|de|Adelstitel|ja|ж°ҸзҲө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noble Titles|qq=}} upon the accession of an emperor{{ill|Tajima no Kimoto|de|Tajima no Kimoto|ja|з”°еі¶е…¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima no Kimoto|qq=}} "The Establishment of Noble Titles вҖ“ Rituals, Service, and Conferment" in {{sfn|еҸІжһ—}} 71-1, [[:en:1988|1988 AD]], p. 61. Among the lower-Ranked officials bearing the Saeki name, there is the {{ill|Ono Family|de|Ono-Familie|ja|е°ҸйҮҺе®¶ (дҪҗдјҜе§“)|lt=Ono Family|lt_ja=е°ҸйҮҺе®¶|lt_de=Ono-Familie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family|qq=}}, which produced the materia medica scholar [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ransan]], and the painter [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Koma]], who founded the {{ill|Kishi School|de|Kishi-Schule|ja|еІёжҙҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi School|qq=}} and served the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]] family, bearing the Saeki name. == Related Clans == === Saeki Tsukuri === '''Saeki Tsukuri''' (-Miyatsuko) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Divine Separation section, and his former name was "Saeki Faction". According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he was a retainer of [[:en:Ichinobe no Oshiwa|Ichinobe Oshi]] and a descendant of {{ill|Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|de|Saeki Fraktion Naka-chiko|ja|дҪҗдјҜйғЁд»Іеӯҗ|lt=Naka-chiko|lt_ja=гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“|lt_de=Naka-chiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|qq=}}In the Pre-accession Annals of [[:en:Emperor YЕ«ryaku|Emperor YЕ«ryaku]], in the 3rd year, October 1 clause of [[:en:Emperor AnkЕҚ|Emperor Ankan]], he is referred to as "Saeki Faction Uruba" (вҖ“uruba), also known as "Naka-teko" (гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“). and, in the 5th year, February 5 clause of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], he was appointed to oversee the Saeki factions from various provinces. === Saeki Obito === '''Saeki Obito''' (-Obito) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] under the {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} category. == Saeki Faction == The Saeki Clan oversaw the central [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]] within the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Official Division]],{{Cite kotobank|Saeki Clan|encyclopedia=Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten 2nd edition|accessdate=2021-12-19}} while the regional Saeki Faction was overseen by {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki Nao|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Nao|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Saeki YЕ«kД“|authorlink=Saeki YЕ«kД“|chapter=The Twelve Palace Gate Names of Miyagi and the Ancient Imperial Attendants Clan|date=April 1963|volume=Research Volume|edition=|title=Study of the New Compilation of Surnames|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|de|Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|ja|еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|qq=}}|isbn=4642021108|ref={{SfnRef|Saeki|1963|}}}} * KЕҚbunkwan|isbn=4642021124|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|language=Japanese|last=Saeki|first=YЕ«kД“|year=1982b|date=July 1982|volume=Textual Verification Volume Part 3|edition=|title=Study of the New revision.comment: Bot: update category вҶ’ Saeki clan
revision.text:
'''Saeki clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) refers to a group of families bearing the name "Saeki" as their [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] (clan name). __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == The Saeki clan traces its origins to {{ill|Amenootsuhiko|ja|еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenootsuhiko|qq=}} (Ame no Oshi-hi no Mikoto), who guided {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|еҪҰзҒ«з“ҠгҖ…жқөе°Ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} during the [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the heavenly grandson]]. The clan split from the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|ЕҢtomo clan]] under the leadership of {{ill|ЕҢtomo no Muroya|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo no Muroya|qq=}}, becoming a {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} (descendant of deities) lineage of {{ill|Tenshin|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin|qq=}} origin. The clan name "Saeki" is believed to derive from their role in guarding the imperial court, as they were seen as those who "block (йҒ® sahe) external threats." This evolved into "Saeki" (гҒ•гҒҲгҒҚ) in modern phonetics, and in some cases as "Saiki" (гҒ•гӮҗгҒҚ). The Saeki clan served as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=}}, leading the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki-be]], who were tasked with guarding the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court of Japan]]. The gates they protected were named "Saeki Gates," though during the Heian period, under {{ill|Tang culture|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang culture|qq=}} influences, the name was changed to "Mokabe Gate" (и—»еЈҒй–Җ). Initially granted the {{ill|Kabane|ja|е§“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of "Muraji" (йҖЈ), the Saeki clan, alongside the ЕҢtomo clan and others, was elevated to the Rank of "Sukune" (е®ҝзҰ°) during the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685 CE). Prominent figures in the Saeki clan included {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|qq=}}, who assisted in the assassination of Soga no Iruka, and {{ill|Saeki no Imaemaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imaemaro|qq=}}, a statesman who served as a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. Military leaders such as {{ill|Saeki no Tsurugite|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Tsurugite|qq=}}, who served under Soga no Umako, and {{ill|Saeki no Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ishiyu|qq=}}, a general during campaigns against the Emishi, were also notable. However, by the end of the Heian period, the clan gradually declined from mid-level aristocracy to {{ill|Chikage|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikage|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}} (bureaucrats of lower Rank). The Saeki name was carried on by families like the Ono family, who produced the herbalist [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ranzan]], and artists such as [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Ganku]] of the Kishi school. == Related Lineages == === Saeki no Miyatsuko (дҪҗдјҜйҖ ) The Saeki no Miyatsuko lineage is documented in the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], appearing under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Shinbetsu. They were originally known as "Saeki-be" and managed the Saeki-be labor group. === Saeki no Obito (дҪҗдјҜйҰ–) The Saeki no Obito line appears in the Shinbetsu of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] as recorded in the Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku. == Saeki-be == The Saeki-be, managed by the central Saeki clan, was a labor group organized as part of the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Shinbetsu]] system. Local Saeki-be groups were supervised by the {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} lineage. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|author=Yuki Saeki|authorlink=дҪҗдјҜжңүжё…|chapter=е®®еҹҺеҚҒдәҢй–ҖиҷҹгҒЁеҸӨд»ЈеӨ©зҡҮиҝ‘дҫҚж°Ҹж—Ҹ|date=1963-4|volume=з ”з©¶зҜҮ|title=ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІгҒ®з ”з©¶|publisher=еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|isbn=464202pp08|ref={{SfnRef|дҪҗдјҜ|1963}}}} * {{Citation|е’Ңжӣё|last=Saeki|first=Yuki|year=1982|date=1982-7|volume=иҖғиӯүзҜҮ з¬¬3|title=ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІгҒ®з ”з©¶|publisher=еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|isbn=464202pp32|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reform]] * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] * {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|ж©ҳеҘҲиүҜйә»е‘ӮгҒ®д№ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} * [[:en:Mount Tate|Tateyama, Toyama]] * [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|Oyama Shrine]] * {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{translated page|ja|дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ|version=102894177|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Saeki Clan]][[sn:Saeki Clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Saeki Clan]] }} [[sn:Saeki Clan]] [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] ==Merged second translation== {{Missing footnotes|date=December 2018}} {{Other uses list|Saeki Clan of the Central Clan|Saeki Clan (ЕҢgami) of Bungo Province|Saeki Clan (Bungo Province)|Saeki Clan of Aki Province|Itsukushima Shinto Priest Family|Saeki Clan of Regional Aristocrats|Saeki Nao|}} {{Japanese clans (Ancient clans) |Clan Name = Saeki Clan |image = |Clan Surname = Saeki [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]] |Ancestor = |Origin = |Clan Founder = {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} |Type = {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}пјҲ{{ill|Heavenly Deity|de|Himmlische Gottheit|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|lt=Heavenly Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©зҘһ|lt_de=Himmlische Gottheit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Deity|qq=}}пјү |Main Lineage = |Notable Figure = {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}} |Descendants = {{ill|Saeki Tsukuri|de|Saeki Tsukuri|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tsukuri|qq=}}
{{ill|Saeki Obito|de|Saeki Obito|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҰ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Obito|qq=}} }} '''Saeki Clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) is a [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] whose name is "Saeki". __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == During the time of [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the Heavenly Grandson]], the ancestor, [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Hikohirakininomikoto]], who was led by {{ill|Amenooshihi|de|Amenooshihi|ja|гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|lt=Heavenly Push Day Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|lt_de=Amenooshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi|qq=}}, (Amenooshihi no Mikoto), is said to be the progenitor, and it is a {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan that split from the Otomo clan during the time of {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ [[:en:Sakyo|Sakyo]], in the Divine Separation section.. After serving the royal authority and undertaking duties such as palace security, there is a theory that they came to be called "Saeki", a term meaning "those who block" (йҒ®гҒҺгӮӢиҖ…), referring to repelling external enemy attacks ([[:en:Historical kana orthography|Historical Kana Usage]] spells it "saheki", while [[:en:Modern kana usage|Modern Kana Usage]] spells it "saeki"). There is also a dialectal pronunciation "saiki" (also "sawiki"). As the central {{ill|BanzЕҚ|de|BanzЕҚ|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/BanzЕҚ|qq=}}, they led the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]], serving the {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|жңқе»· (ж—Ҙжң¬)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja=жңқе»·|lt_de=Kaiserhof|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|qq=}} as palace gate guards and as a military force. Incidentally, the palace gate in charge of security was named {{ill|Saeki Gate|de|Saeki-Tor|ja|дҪҗдјҜй–Җ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Gate|qq=}} after the clan name, but at the {{ill|Heian Palace|de|Heian-Palast|ja|е№іе®үе®®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Palace|qq=}}, under the influence of {{ill|Tang-style Culture|de|Tang-Kultur|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-style Culture|qq=}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Moss Wall Gate|de|Mooswandtor|ja|и—»еЈҒй–Җ|lt=Moss Wall Gate (SЕҚheki Gate)|lt_ja=и—»еЈҒпјҲгҒқгҒҶгҒёгҒҚпјүй–Җ|lt_de=Mooswandtor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moss Wall Gate|qq=}}вҖҳ[[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiriyaku]]вҖҷ [[:en:KЕҚnin (era)|KЕҚnin]] 9th year ([[:en:818|818 AD]]) April 27 clause.. The [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] was originally [[:en:Muraji|Ren]], but in the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] ([[:en:685|685 AD]]), together with members of the same group such as the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], they were granted the surname [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]]. In the {{ill|Main Palace Office|de|Hauptpalastamt|ja|дё»ж®ҝеҜ®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace Office|qq=}}, the name of the Saeki faction remains as one of the noble divisions. Notable figures include {{ill|Saeki Komaro|de|Saeki Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Komaro|qq=}}, who, following {{ill|Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|de|Prinz Nakano-ЕҢe|ja|дёӯеӨ§е…„зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|qq=}}, assassinated [[:en:Soga no Iruka|Soga no Iruka]], and {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}}, who was active in government from the Nara to the Heian period and held positions such as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Mikoto|de|Dazai no Mikoto|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Mikoto|qq=}}. The clan also produced many warriors, including {{ill|Saeki Tankeide|de|Saeki Tankeide|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tankeide|qq=}} (the father of Komaro), who, following [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], executed {{ill|Prince Anahobe|de|Prinz Anahobe|ja|з©ҙз©ӮйғЁзҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anahobe|qq=}}; {{ill|Saeki Ishiyu|de|Saeki Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ishiyu|qq=}}, the Ezo General after the conquest of Echigo; and {{ill|Saeki Katsuragi|de|Saeki Katsuragi|ja|дҪҗдјҜи‘ӣеҹҺ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Katsuragi|qq=}} (the grandson of Ishiyu), the Deputy General of the Eastern Expedition. Apart from Imao Hito, no one ascended to the position of {{ill|Giseikan|de|Giseikan|ja|иӯ°ж”ҝе®ҳ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giseikan|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}, and the clan eventually declined from being mid-level nobles to lower-Ranked officials ({{ill|Underground Officials|de|Underground Officials|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|lt=Underground Officials|lt_ja=ең°дёӢе®ҳдәә|lt_de=Underground Officials|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}}). Until around the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku Period]], together with the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], {{ill|Wake Clan|de|Wake Clan|ja|е’Ңж°—ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Clan|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi clan|Baekje Royal Family]], the clan was considered one that received {{ill|Noble Titles|de|Adelstitel|ja|ж°ҸзҲө|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noble Titles|qq=}} upon the accession of an emperor{{ill|Tajima no Kimoto|de|Tajima no Kimoto|ja|з”°еі¶е…¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima no Kimoto|qq=}} "The Establishment of Noble Titles вҖ“ Rituals, Service, and Conferment" in {{sfn|еҸІжһ—}} 71-1, [[:en:1988|1988 AD]], p. 61. Among the lower-Ranked officials bearing the Saeki name, there is the {{ill|Ono Family|de|Ono-Familie|ja|е°ҸйҮҺе®¶ (дҪҗдјҜе§“)|lt=Ono Family|lt_ja=е°ҸйҮҺе®¶|lt_de=Ono-Familie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family|qq=}}, which produced the materia medica scholar [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ransan]], and the painter [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Koma]], who founded the {{ill|Kishi School|de|Kishi-Schule|ja|еІёжҙҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi School|qq=}} and served the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]] family, bearing the Saeki name. == Related Clans == === Saeki Tsukuri === '''Saeki Tsukuri''' (-Miyatsuko) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Divine Separation section, and his former name was "Saeki Faction". According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he was a retainer of [[:en:Ichinobe no Oshiwa|Ichinobe Oshi]] and a descendant of {{ill|Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|de|Saeki Fraktion Naka-chiko|ja|дҪҗдјҜйғЁд»Іеӯҗ|lt=Naka-chiko|lt_ja=гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“|lt_de=Naka-chiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|qq=}}In the Pre-accession Annals of [[:en:Emperor YЕ«ryaku|Emperor YЕ«ryaku]], in the 3rd year, October 1 clause of [[:en:Emperor AnkЕҚ|Emperor Ankan]], he is referred to as "Saeki Faction Uruba" (вҖ“uruba), also known as "Naka-teko" (гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“). and, in the 5th year, February 5 clause of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], he was appointed to oversee the Saeki factions from various provinces. === Saeki Obito === '''Saeki Obito''' (-Obito) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] under the {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} category. == Saeki Faction == The Saeki Clan oversaw the central [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]] within the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Official Division]],{{Cite kotobank|Saeki Clan|encyclopedia=Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten 2nd edition|accessdate=2021-12-19}} while the regional Saeki Faction was overseen by {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki Nao|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Nao|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}. == Footnotes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Saeki YЕ«kД“|authorlink=Saeki YЕ«kД“|chapter=The Twelve Palace Gate Names of Miyagi and the Ancient Imperial Attendants Clan|date=April 1963|volume=Research Volume|edition=|title=Study of the New Compilation of Surnames|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|de|Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|ja|еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkwan|qq=}}|isbn=4642021108|ref={{SfnRef|Saeki|1963|}}}} * KЕҚbunkwan|isbn=4642021124|ref=harv}} * {{Citation|language=Japanese|last=Saeki|first=YЕ«kД“|year=1982b|date=July 1982|volume=Textual Verification Volume Part 3|edition=|title=Study of the New revision.text:
'''Saeki clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) refers to a group of families bearing the name "Saeki" as their [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] (clan name). __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == The Saeki clan traces its origins to {{ill|Amenootsuhiko|ja|еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenootsuhiko|qq=}} (Ame no Oshi-hi no Mikoto), who guided {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|еҪҰзҒ«з“ҠгҖ…жқөе°Ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} during the [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the heavenly grandson]]. The clan split from the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|ЕҢtomo clan]] under the leadership of {{ill|ЕҢtomo no Muroya|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo no Muroya|qq=}}, becoming a {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} (descendant of deities) lineage of {{ill|Tenshin|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin|qq=}} origin. The clan name "Saeki" is believed to derive from their role in guarding the imperial court, as they were seen as those who "block (йҒ® sahe) external threats." This evolved into "Saeki" (гҒ•гҒҲгҒҚ) in modern phonetics, and in some cases as "Saiki" (гҒ•гӮҗгҒҚ). The Saeki clan served as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=}}, leading the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki-be]], who were tasked with guarding the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court of Japan]]. The gates they protected were named "Saeki Gates," though during the Heian period, under {{ill|Tang culture|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang culture|qq=}} influences, the name was changed to "Mokabe Gate" (и—»еЈҒй–Җ). Initially granted the {{ill|Kabane|ja|е§“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of "Muraji" (йҖЈ), the Saeki clan, alongside the ЕҢtomo clan and others, was elevated to the Rank of "Sukune" (е®ҝзҰ°) during the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685 CE). Prominent figures in the Saeki clan included {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|qq=}}, who assisted in the assassination of Soga no Iruka, and {{ill|Saeki no Imaemaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imaemaro|qq=}}, a statesman who served as a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. Military leaders such as {{ill|Saeki no Tsurugite|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Tsurugite|qq=}}, who served under Soga no Umako, and {{ill|Saeki no Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ishiyu|qq=}}, a general during campaigns against the Emishi, were also notable. However, by the end of the Heian period, the clan gradually declined from mid-level aristocracy to {{ill|Chikage|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikage|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}} (bureaucrats of lower Rank). The Saeki name was carried on by families like the Ono family, who produced the herbalist [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ranzan]], and artists such as [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Ganku]] of the Kishi school. == Related Lineages == === Saeki no Miyatsuko (дҪҗдјҜйҖ ) The Saeki no Miyatsuko lineage is documented in the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], appearing under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Shinbetsu. They were originally known as "Saeki-be" and managed the Saeki-be labor group. === Saeki no Obito (дҪҗдјҜйҰ–) The Saeki no Obito line appears in the Shinbetsu of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] as recorded in the Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku. == Saeki-be == The Saeki-be, managed by the central Saeki clan, was a labor group organized as part of the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Shinbetsu]] system. Local Saeki-be groups were supervised by the {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} lineage. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|е’Ңжӣё|author=Yuki Saeki|authorlink=дҪҗдјҜжңүжё…|chapter=е®®еҹҺеҚҒдәҢй–ҖиҷҹгҒЁеҸӨд»ЈеӨ©зҡҮиҝ‘дҫҚж°Ҹж—Ҹ|date=1963-4|volume=з ”з©¶зҜҮ|title=ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІгҒ®з ”з©¶|publisher=еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|isbn=464202pp08|ref={{SfnRef|дҪҗдјҜ|1963}}}} * {{Citation|е’Ңжӣё|last=Saeki|first=Yuki|year=1982|date=1982-7|volume=иҖғиӯүзҜҮ з¬¬3|title=ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІгҒ®з ”з©¶|publisher=еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ|isbn=464202pp32|ref=harv}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reform]] * [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]] * [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion]] * {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|ж©ҳеҘҲиүҜйә»е‘ӮгҒ®д№ұ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} * [[:en:Mount Tate|Tateyama, Toyama]] * [[:en:Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|Oyama Shrine]] * {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{translated page|ja|дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ|version=102894177|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Saeki Clan]][[sn:Saeki Clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:Saeki Clan]] }} [[sn:Saeki Clan]] [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] ==Merged second translation== {{Missing footnotes|date=December 2018}} {{Other uses list|Saeki Clan of the Central Clan|Saeki Clan (ЕҢgami) of Bungo Province|Saeki Clan (Bungo Province)|Saeki Clan of Aki Province|Itsukushima Shinto Priest Family|Saeki Clan of Regional Aristocrats|Saeki Nao|}} {{Japanese clans (Ancient clans) |Clan Name = Saeki Clan |image = |Clan Surname = Saeki [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]] |Ancestor = |Origin = |Clan Founder = {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} |Type = {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}пјҲ{{ill|Heavenly Deity|de|Himmlische Gottheit|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|lt=Heavenly Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©зҘһ|lt_de=Himmlische Gottheit|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heavenly Deity|qq=}}пјү |Main Lineage = |Notable Figure = {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}} |Descendants = {{ill|Saeki Tsukuri|de|Saeki Tsukuri|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tsukuri|qq=}}
{{ill|Saeki Obito|de|Saeki Obito|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҰ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Obito|qq=}} }} '''Saeki Clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) is a [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] whose name is "Saeki". __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == During the time of [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the Heavenly Grandson]], the ancestor, [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Hikohirakininomikoto]], who was led by {{ill|Amenooshihi|de|Amenooshihi|ja|гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|lt=Heavenly Push Day Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|lt_de=Amenooshihi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi|qq=}}, (Amenooshihi no Mikoto), is said to be the progenitor, and it is a {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan that split from the Otomo clan during the time of {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ [[:en:Sakyo|Sakyo]], in the Divine Separation section.. After serving the royal authority and undertaking duties such as palace security, there is a theory that they came to be called "Saeki", a term meaning "those who block" (йҒ®гҒҺгӮӢиҖ…), referring to repelling external enemy attacks ([[:en:Historical kana orthography|Historical Kana Usage]] spells it "saheki", while [[:en:Modern kana usage|Modern Kana Usage]] spells it "saeki"). There is also a dialectal pronunciation "saiki" (also "sawiki"). As the central {{ill|BanzЕҚ|de|BanzЕҚ|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/BanzЕҚ|qq=}}, they led the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]], serving the {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|жңқе»· (ж—Ҙжң¬)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja=жңқе»·|lt_de=Kaiserhof|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|qq=}} as palace gate guards and as a military force. Incidentally, the palace gate in charge of security was named {{ill|Saeki Gate|de|Saeki-Tor|ja|дҪҗдјҜй–Җ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Gate|qq=}} after the clan name, but at the [[:en:Heian Palace|Heian Palace]], under the influence of {{ill|Tang-style Culture|de|Tang-Kultur|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-style Culture|qq=}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Moss Wall Gate|de|Mooswandtor|ja|и—»еЈҒй–Җ|lt=Moss Wall Gate (SЕҚheki Gate)|lt_ja=и—»еЈҒпјҲгҒқгҒҶгҒёгҒҚпјүй–Җ|lt_de=Mooswandtor|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moss Wall Gate|qq=}}вҖҳ[[:en:Nihon Kiryaku|Nihon Kiriyaku]]вҖҷ [[:en:KЕҚnin (era)|KЕҚnin]] 9th year ([[:en:818|818 AD]]) April 27 clause.. The [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] was originally [[:en:Muraji|Ren]], but in the 13th year of [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] ([[:en:685|685 AD]]), together with members of the same group such as the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], they were granted the surname [[:en:Sukune|Sukunabe]]. In the {{ill|Main Palace Office|de|Hauptpalastamt|ja|дё»ж®ҝеҜ®|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace Office|qq=}}, the name of the Saeki faction remains as one of the noble divisions. Notable figures include {{ill|Saeki Komaro|de|Saeki Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Komaro|qq=}}, who, following {{ill|Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|de|Prinz Nakano-ЕҢe|ja|дёӯеӨ§е…„зҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|qq=}}, assassinated [[:en:Soga no Iruka|Soga no Iruka]], and {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|qq=}}, who was active in government from the Nara to the Heian period and held positions such as [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Mikoto|de|Dazai no Mikoto|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Mikoto|qq=}}. The clan also produced many warriors, including {{ill|Saeki Tankeide|de|Saeki Tankeide|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tankeide|qq=}} (the father of Komaro), who, following [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga no Umako]], executed {{ill|Prince Anahobe|de|Prinz Anahobe|ja|з©ҙз©ӮйғЁзҡҮеӯҗ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anahobe|qq=}}; {{ill|Saeki Ishiyu|de|Saeki Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ishiyu|qq=}}, the Ezo General after the conquest of Echigo; and {{ill|Saeki Katsuragi|de|Saeki Katsuragi|ja|дҪҗдјҜи‘ӣеҹҺ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Katsuragi|qq=}} (the grandson of Ishiyu), the Deputy General of the Eastern Expedition. Apart from Imao Hito, no one ascended to the position of {{ill|Giseikan|de|Giseikan|ja|иӯ°ж”ҝе®ҳ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giseikan|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}, and the clan eventually declined from being mid-level nobles to lower-Ranked officials ({{ill|Underground Officials|de|Underground Officials|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|lt=Underground Officials|lt_ja=ең°дёӢе®ҳдәә|lt_de=Underground Officials|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}}). Until around the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku Period]], together with the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|Otomo Clan]], {{ill|Wake Clan|de|Wake Clan|ja|е’Ңж°—ж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Clan|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Kudara no Konikishi clan|Baekje Royal Family]], the clan was considered one that received [[:en:Imperial, royal and noble ranks|Noble Titles]] upon the accession of an emperor{{ill|Tajima no Kimoto|de|Tajima no Kimoto|ja|з”°еі¶е…¬|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima no Kimoto|qq=}} "The Establishment of Noble Titles вҖ“ Rituals, Service, and Conferment" in {{sfn|еҸІжһ—}} 71-1, [[:en:1988|1988 AD]], p. 61. Among the lower-Ranked officials bearing the Saeki name, there is the {{ill|Ono Family|de|Ono-Familie|ja|е°ҸйҮҺе®¶ (дҪҗдјҜе§“)|lt=Ono Family|lt_ja=е°ҸйҮҺе®¶|lt_de=Ono-Familie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family|qq=}}, which produced the materia medica scholar [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ransan]], and the painter [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Koma]], who founded the {{ill|Kishi School|de|Kishi-Schule|ja|еІёжҙҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi School|qq=}} and served the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]] family, bearing the Saeki name. == Related Clans == === Saeki Tsukuri === '''Saeki Tsukuri''' (-Miyatsuko) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Divine Separation section, and his former name was "Saeki Faction". According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], he was a retainer of [[:en:Ichinobe no Oshiwa|Ichinobe Oshi]] and a descendant of {{ill|Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|de|Saeki Fraktion Naka-chiko|ja|дҪҗдјҜйғЁд»Іеӯҗ|lt=Naka-chiko|lt_ja=гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“|lt_de=Naka-chiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|qq=}}In the Pre-accession Annals of [[:en:Emperor YЕ«ryaku|Emperor YЕ«ryaku]], in the 3rd year, October 1 clause of [[:en:Emperor AnkЕҚ|Emperor Ankan]], he is referred to as "Saeki Faction Uruba" (вҖ“uruba), also known as "Naka-teko" (гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“). and, in the 5th year, February 5 clause of [[:en:Emperor Ninken|Emperor Ninken]], he was appointed to oversee the Saeki factions from various provinces. === Saeki Obito === '''Saeki Obito''' (-Obito) appears in the вҖҳ[[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|New Compilation of Surnames]]вҖҷ in [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] under the {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} category. == Saeki Faction == The Saeki Clan oversaw the central [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki Faction]] within the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Official Division]],{{Cite kotobank|Saeki Clan|encyclopedia=Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten 2nd edition|accessdate=2021-12-19}} while the regional Saeki Faction was overseen by {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki Nao|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Nao|qq=}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}. == Footnotes == '''Saeki clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) refers to a group of families bearing the name "Saeki" as their [[:en:Uji (clan)|Uji]] (clan name). __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == The Saeki clan traces its origins to {{ill|Amenootsuhiko|ja|еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenootsuhiko|qq=}} (Ame no Oshi-hi no Mikoto), who guided {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|еҪҰзҒ«з“ҠгҖ…жқөе°Ҡ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} during the [[:en:Tenson kЕҚrin|Descent of the heavenly grandson]]. The clan split from the [[:en:ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|ЕҢtomo clan]] under the leadership of {{ill|ЕҢtomo no Muroya|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo no Muroya|qq=}}, becoming a {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} (descendant of deities) lineage of {{ill|Tenshin|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin|qq=}} origin. The clan name "Saeki" is believed to derive from their role in guarding the imperial court, as they were seen as those who "block (йҒ® sahe) external threats." This evolved into "Saeki" (гҒ•гҒҲгҒҚ) in modern phonetics, and in some cases as "Saiki" (гҒ•гӮҗгҒҚ). The Saeki clan served as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|дјҙйҖ |qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=}}, leading the [[:en:Saeki people|Saeki-be]], who were tasked with guarding the [[:en:Royal court|Imperial Court of Japan]]. The gates they protected were named "Saeki Gates," though during the Heian period, under {{ill|Tang culture|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang culture|qq=}} influences, the name was changed to "Mokabe Gate" (и—»еЈҒй–Җ). Initially granted the {{ill|Kabane|ja|е§“|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|qq=}} of "Muraji" (йҖЈ), the Saeki clan, alongside the ЕҢtomo clan and others, was elevated to the Rank of "Sukune" (е®ҝзҰ°) during the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685 CE). Prominent figures in the Saeki clan included {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|qq=}}, who assisted in the assassination of Soga no Iruka, and {{ill|Saeki no Imaemaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imaemaro|qq=}}, a statesman who served as a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]] and {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|qq=}}. Military leaders such as {{ill|Saeki no Tsurugite|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Tsurugite|qq=}}, who served under Soga no Umako, and {{ill|Saeki no Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ishiyu|qq=}}, a general during campaigns against the Emishi, were also notable. However, by the end of the Heian period, the clan gradually declined from mid-level aristocracy to {{ill|Chikage|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikage|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|qq=}} (bureaucrats of lower Rank). The Saeki name was carried on by families like the Ono family, who produced the herbalist [[:en:Ono Ranzan|Ono Ranzan]], and artists such as [[:en:Kishi Ganku|Kishi Ganku]] of the Kishi school. == Related Lineages == === Saeki no Miyatsuko (дҪҗдјҜйҖ ) The Saeki no Miyatsuko lineage is documented in the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], appearing under the [[:en:Ukyo|Right Capital]] Shinbetsu. They were originally known as "Saeki-be" and managed the Saeki-be labor group. === Saeki no Obito (дҪҗдјҜйҰ–) The Saeki no Obito line appears in the Shinbetsu of [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] as recorded in the Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku. == Saeki-be == The Saeki-be, managed by the central Saeki clan, was a labor group organized as part of the [[:en:Shinabe clans|Shinbetsu]] system. Local Saeki-be groups were supervised by the {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|qq=}} lineage. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite
