Tsukuri|de|Saeki Tsukuri|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tsukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Saeki Obito|de|Saeki Obito|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҰ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Obito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Saeki Clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) is a {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji|1=Uji (clan)}} whose name is "Saeki". __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == During the time of {{ill|Tenson kЕҚrin|en|Tenson kЕҚrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandson|1=Tenson kЕҚrin}}, the ancestor, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Hikohirakininomikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, who was led by {{ill|Amenooshihi|de|Amenooshihi|ja|гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|lt=Heavenly Push Day Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|lt_de=Amenooshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939616|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|2=ja|3=гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|4=zh|5=еӨ©еҝҚж—Ҙе‘Ҫ}}, (Amenooshihi no Mikoto), is said to be the progenitor, and it is a {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan that split from the Otomo clan during the time of {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|1=ЕҢtomo Muroya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432336}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ {{ill|Sakyo|en|Sakyo|WD=Q11479660|lt=Sakyo|1=Sakyo}}, in the Divine Separation section.. After serving the royal authority and undertaking duties such as palace security, there is a theory that they came to be called "Saeki", a term meaning "those who block" (йҒ®гҒҺгӮӢиҖ…), referring to repelling external enemy attacks ({{ill|Historical kana orthography|en|Historical kana orthography|WD=Q1142552|lt=Historical Kana Usage|1=Historical kana orthography}} spells it "saheki", while {{ill|Modern kana usage|en|Modern kana usage|WD=Q2572881|lt=Modern Kana Usage|1=Modern kana usage}} spells it "saeki"). There is also a dialectal pronunciation "saiki" (also "sawiki"). As the central {{ill|BanzЕҚ|de|BanzЕҚ|ja|дјҙйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/BanzЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomonomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}}, they led the {{ill|Saeki people|en|Saeki people|WD=Q7398288|lt=Saeki Faction|1=Saeki people}}, serving the {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|жңқе»· (ж—Ҙжң¬)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja=жңқе»·|lt_de=Kaiserhof|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Imperial Court of Japan|WD=Q30253177}} as palace gate guards and as a military force. Incidentally, the palace gate in charge of security was named {{ill|Saeki Gate|de|Saeki-Tor|ja|дҪҗдјҜй–Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Gate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и—»еЈҒй–Җ|1=SЕҚhekimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17995614}} after the clan name, but at the {{ill|Heian Palace|en|Heian Palace|WD=Q304501|lt=Heian Palace|1=Heian Palace}}, under the influence of {{ill|Tang-style Culture|de|Tang-Kultur|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-style Culture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е№іж–ҮеҢ–|1=TenpyЕҚ culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5525117}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Moss Wall Gate|de|Mooswandtor|ja|и—»еЈҒй–Җ|lt=Moss Wall Gate (SЕҚheki Gate)|lt_ja=и—»еЈҒпјҲгҒқгҒҶгҒёгҒҚпјүй–Җ|lt_de=Mooswandtor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moss Wall Gate|1=SЕҚhekimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17995614}}вҖҳ{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiriyaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}вҖҷ {{ill|KЕҚnin (era)|en|KЕҚnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=KЕҚnin|1=KЕҚnin (era)}} 9th year ({{ill|818|en|818|WD=Q25083|lt=818 AD|1=818}}) April 27 clause.. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} was originally {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}, but in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685 AD|1=685}}), together with members of the same group such as the {{ill|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|en|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Otomo Clan|1=ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)}}, they were granted the surname {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukunabe|1=Sukune}}. In the {{ill|Main Palace Office|de|Hauptpalastamt|ja|дё»ж®ҝеҜ®|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace Office|1=ShudenryЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368767}}, the name of the Saeki faction remains as one of the noble divisions. Notable figures include {{ill|Saeki Komaro|de|Saeki Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Komaro|1=Saeki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382809}}, who, following {{ill|Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|de|Prinz Nakano-ЕҢe|ja|дёӯеӨ§е…„зҡҮеӯҗ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©жҷәеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q314802}}, assassinated {{ill|Soga no Iruka|en|Soga no Iruka|WD=Q736621|lt=Soga no Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}, and {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|1=Saeki no Imaemishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382787}}, who was active in government from the Nara to the Heian period and held positions such as {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} and {{ill|Dazai no Mikoto|de|Dazai no Mikoto|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§е®°еёҘ|1=Dazai no Sochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434484}}. The clan also produced many warriors, including {{ill|Saeki Tankeide|de|Saeki Tankeide|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tankeide|1=Saeki no Nifute|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828498}} (the father of Komaro), who, following {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}, executed {{ill|Prince Anahobe|de|Prinz Anahobe|ja|з©ҙз©ӮйғЁзҡҮеӯҗ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anahobe|qq=}}; {{ill|Saeki Ishiyu|de|Saeki Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ishiyu|1=Saeki no Iwayu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382879}}, the Ezo General after the conquest of Echigo; and {{ill|Saeki Katsuragi|de|Saeki Katsuragi|ja|дҪҗдјҜи‘ӣеҹҺ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Katsuragi|1=Saeki no Kazuragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382895}} (the grandson of Ishiyu), the Deputy General of the Eastern Expedition. Apart from Imao Hito, no one ascended to the position of {{ill|Giseikan|de|Giseikan|ja|иӯ°ж”ҝе®ҳ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giseikan|1=Giseikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632467}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}, and the clan eventually declined from being mid-level nobles to lower-Ranked officials ({{ill|Underground Officials|de|Underground Officials|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|lt=Underground Officials|lt_ja=ең°дёӢе®ҳдәә|lt_de=Underground Officials|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}). Until around the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku Period|1=Sengoku period}}, together with the {{ill|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|en|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Otomo Clan|1=ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)}}, {{ill|Wake Clan|de|Wake Clan|ja|е’Ңж°—ж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Clan|1=Wake clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417729}}, and the {{ill|Kudara no Konikishi clan|en|Kudara no Konikishi clan|WD=Q710197|lt=Baekje Royal Family|1=Kudara no Konikishi clan}}, the clan was considered one that received {{ill|Imperial, royal and noble ranks|en|Imperial, royal and noble ranks|WD=Q355567|lt=Noble Titles|1=Imperial, royal and noble ranks}} upon the accession of an emperor{{ill|Tajima no Kimoto|de|Tajima no Kimoto|ja|з”°еі¶е…¬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima no Kimoto|1=Isao Tajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45819717}} "The Establishment of Noble Titles вҖ“ Rituals, Service, and Conferment" in {{sfn|еҸІжһ—}} 71-1, {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988 AD|1=1988}}, p. 61. Among the lower-Ranked officials bearing the Saeki name, there is the {{ill|Ono Family|de|Ono-Familie|ja|е°ҸйҮҺе®¶ (дҪҗдјҜе§“)|lt=Ono Family|lt_ja=е°ҸйҮҺе®¶|lt_de=Ono-Familie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which produced the materia medica scholar {{ill|Ono Ranzan|en|Ono Ranzan|WD=Q11464264|lt=Ono Ransan|1=Ono Ranzan}}, and the painter {{ill|Kishi Ganku|en|Kishi Ganku|WD=Q555061|lt=Kishi Koma|1=Kishi Ganku}}, who founded the {{ill|Kishi School|de|Kishi-Schule|ja|еІёжҙҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi School|1=Kishi school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475951}} and served the {{ill|Arisugawa-no-miya|en|Arisugawa-no-miya|WD=Q2861427|lt=Arisugawa-no-miya|1=Arisugawa-no-miya}} family, bearing the Saeki name. == Related Clans == === Saeki Tsukuri === '''Saeki Tsukuri''' (-Miyatsuko) appears in the вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ under the {{ill|Ukyo|en|Ukyo|WD=Q2474484|lt=Right Capital|1=Ukyo}} Divine Separation section, and his former name was "Saeki Faction". According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he was a retainer of {{ill|Ichinobe no Oshiwa|en|Ichinobe no Oshiwa|WD=Q2297842|lt=Ichinobe Oshi|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}} and a descendant of {{ill|Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|de|Saeki Fraktion Naka-chiko|ja|дҪҗдјҜйғЁд»Іеӯҗ|lt=Naka-chiko|lt_ja=гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“|lt_de=Naka-chiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҪҗдјҜйғЁеЈІијӘ|1=Saekibe no Uruwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876437}}In the Pre-accession Annals of {{ill|Emperor YЕ«ryaku|en|Emperor YЕ«ryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor YЕ«ryaku|1=Emperor YЕ«ryaku}}, in the 3rd year, October 1 clause of {{ill|Emperor AnkЕҚ|en|Emperor AnkЕҚ|WD=Q361010|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor AnkЕҚ}}, he is referred to as "Saeki Faction Uruba" (вҖ“uruba), also known as "Naka-teko" (гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“). and, in the 5th year, February 5 clause of {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}}, he was appointed to oversee the Saeki factions from various provinces. === Saeki Obito === '''Saeki Obito''' (-Obito) appears in the вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} under the {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} category. == Saeki Faction == The Saeki Clan oversaw the central {{ill|Saeki people|en|Saeki people|WD=Q7398288|lt=Saeki Faction|1=Saeki people}} within the {{ill|Shinabe clans|en|Shinabe clans|WD=Q11418456|lt=Official Division|1=Shinabe clans}},{{Cite kotobank|Saeki Clan|encyclopedia=Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten 2nd edition|accessdate=2021-12-19}} while the regional Saeki Faction was overseen by {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki Nao|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Nao|1=Nao Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382874}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}. == [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] '''Saeki clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) refers to a group of families bearing the name "Saeki" as their {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji|1=Uji (clan)}} (clan name). __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == The Saeki clan traces its origins to {{ill|Amenootsuhiko|ja|еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenootsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939616|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|2=ja|3=гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|4=zh|5=еӨ©еҝҚж—Ҙе‘Ҫ}} (Ame no Oshi-hi no Mikoto), who guided {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|еҪҰзҒ«з“ҠгҖ…жқөе°Ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} during the {{ill|Tenson kЕҚrin|en|Tenson kЕҚrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the heavenly grandson|1=Tenson kЕҚrin}}. The clan split from the {{ill|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|en|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=ЕҢtomo clan|1=ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)}} under the leadership of {{ill|ЕҢtomo no Muroya|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo no Muroya|1=ЕҢtomo Muroya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432336}}, becoming a {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} (descendant of deities) lineage of {{ill|Tenshin|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}} origin. The clan name "Saeki" is believed to derive from their role in guarding the imperial court, as they were seen as those who "block (йҒ® sahe) external threats." This evolved into "Saeki" (гҒ•гҒҲгҒҚ) in modern phonetics, and in some cases as "Saiki" (гҒ•гӮҗгҒҚ). The Saeki clan served as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|дјҙйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, leading the {{ill|Saeki people|en|Saeki people|WD=Q7398288|lt=Saeki-be|1=Saeki people}}, who were tasked with guarding the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court of Japan|1=Royal court}}. The gates they protected were named "Saeki Gates," though during the Heian period, under {{ill|Tang culture|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang culture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е№іж–ҮеҢ–|1=TenpyЕҚ culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5525117}} influences, the name was changed to "Mokabe Gate" (и—»еЈҒй–Җ). Initially granted the {{ill|Kabane|ja|е§“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of "Muraji" (йҖЈ), the Saeki clan, alongside the ЕҢtomo clan and others, was elevated to the Rank of "Sukune" (е®ҝзҰ°) during the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685 CE). Prominent figures in the Saeki clan included {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|1=Saeki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382809}}, who assisted in the assassination of Soga no Iruka, and {{ill|Saeki no Imaemaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imaemaro|1=Saeki no Imaemishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382787}}, a statesman who served as a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} and {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§е®°еёҘ}}. Military leaders such as {{ill|Saeki no Tsurugite|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Tsurugite|1=Saeki no Nifute|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828498}}, who served under Soga no Umako, and {{ill|Saeki no Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ishiyu|1=Saeki no Iwayu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382879}}, a general during campaigns against the Emishi, were also notable. However, by the end of the Heian period, the clan gradually declined from mid-level aristocracy to {{ill|Chikage|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikage|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}} (bureaucrats of lower Rank). The Saeki name was carried on by families like the Ono family, who produced the herbalist {{ill|Ono Ranzan|en|Ono Ranzan|WD=Q11464264|lt=Ono Ranzan|1=Ono Ranzan}}, and artists such as {{ill|Kishi Ganku|en|Kishi Ganku|WD=Q555061|lt=Kishi Ganku|1=Kishi Ganku}} of the Kishi school. == Related Lineages == === Saeki no Miyatsuko (дҪҗдјҜйҖ ) The Saeki no Miyatsuko lineage is documented in the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}, appearing under the {{ill|Ukyo|en|Ukyo|WD=Q2474484|lt=Right Capital|1=Ukyo}} Shinbetsu. They were originally known as "Saeki-be" and managed the Saeki-be labor group. === Saeki no Obito (дҪҗдјҜйҰ–) The Saeki no Obito line appears in the Shinbetsu of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} as recorded in the Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku. == Saeki-be == The Saeki-be, managed by the central Saeki clan, was a labor group organized as part of the {{ill|Shinabe clans|en|Shinabe clans|WD=Q11418456|lt=Shinbetsu|1=Shinabe clans}} system. Local Saeki-be groups were supervised by the {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|1=Nao Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382874}} lineage. == Tsukuri|de|Saeki Tsukuri|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tsukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Saeki Obito|de|Saeki Obito|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҰ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Obito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Saeki Clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) is a {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji|1=Uji (clan)}} whose name is "Saeki". __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == During the time of {{ill|Tenson kЕҚrin|en|Tenson kЕҚrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandson|1=Tenson kЕҚrin}}, the ancestor, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Hikohirakininomikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, who was led by {{ill|Amenooshihi|de|Amenooshihi|ja|гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|lt=Heavenly Push Day Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|lt_de=Amenooshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939616|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|2=ja|3=гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|4=zh|5=еӨ©еҝҚж—Ҙе‘Ҫ}}, (Amenooshihi no Mikoto), is said to be the progenitor, and it is a {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan that split from the Otomo clan during the time of {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|1=ЕҢtomo Muroya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432336}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ {{ill|Sakyo|en|Sakyo|WD=Q11479660|lt=Sakyo|1=Sakyo}}, in the Divine Separation section.. After serving the royal authority and undertaking duties such as palace security, there is a theory that they came to be called "Saeki", a term meaning "those who block" (йҒ®гҒҺгӮӢиҖ…), referring to repelling external enemy attacks ({{ill|Historical kana orthography|en|Historical kana orthography|WD=Q1142552|lt=Historical Kana Usage|1=Historical kana orthography}} spells it "saheki", while {{ill|Modern kana usage|en|Modern kana usage|WD=Q2572881|lt=Modern Kana Usage|1=Modern kana usage}} spells it "saeki"). There is also a dialectal pronunciation "saiki" (also "sawiki"). As the central {{ill|BanzЕҚ|de|BanzЕҚ|ja|дјҙйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/BanzЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomonomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}}, they led the {{ill|Saeki people|en|Saeki people|WD=Q7398288|lt=Saeki Faction|1=Saeki people}}, serving the {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|жңқе»· (ж—Ҙжң¬)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja=жңқе»·|lt_de=Kaiserhof|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Imperial Court of Japan|WD=Q30253177}} as palace gate guards and as a military force. Incidentally, the palace gate in charge of security was named {{ill|Saeki Gate|de|Saeki-Tor|ja|дҪҗдјҜй–Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Gate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и—»еЈҒй–Җ|1=SЕҚhekimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17995614}} after the clan name, but at the {{ill|Heian Palace|en|Heian Palace|WD=Q304501|lt=Heian Palace|1=Heian Palace}}, under the influence of {{ill|Tang-style Culture|de|Tang-Kultur|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-style Culture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е№іж–ҮеҢ–|1=TenpyЕҚ culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5525117}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Moss Wall Gate|de|Mooswandtor|ja|и—»еЈҒй–Җ|lt=Moss Wall Gate (SЕҚheki Gate)|lt_ja=и—»еЈҒпјҲгҒқгҒҶгҒёгҒҚпјүй–Җ|lt_de=Mooswandtor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moss Wall Gate|1=SЕҚhekimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17995614}}вҖҳ{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiriyaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}вҖҷ {{ill|KЕҚnin (era)|en|KЕҚnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=KЕҚnin|1=KЕҚnin (era)}} 9th year ({{ill|818|en|818|WD=Q25083|lt=818 AD|1=818}}) April 27 clause.. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} was originally {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}, but in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685 AD|1=685}}), together with members of the same group such as the {{ill|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|en|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Otomo Clan|1=ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)}}, they were granted the surname {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukunabe|1=Sukune}}. In the {{ill|Main Palace Office|de|Hauptpalastamt|ja|дё»ж®ҝеҜ®|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace Office|1=ShudenryЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368767}}, the name of the Saeki faction remains as one of the noble divisions. Notable figures include {{ill|Saeki Komaro|de|Saeki Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Komaro|1=Saeki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382809}}, who, following {{ill|Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|de|Prinz Nakano-ЕҢe|ja|дёӯеӨ§е…„зҡҮеӯҗ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©жҷәеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q314802}}, assassinated {{ill|Soga no Iruka|en|Soga no Iruka|WD=Q736621|lt=Soga no Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}, and {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|1=Saeki no Imaemishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382787}}, who was active in government from the Nara to the Heian period and held positions such as {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} and {{ill|Dazai no Mikoto|de|Dazai no Mikoto|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§е®°еёҘ|1=Dazai no Sochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434484}}. The clan also produced many warriors, including {{ill|Saeki Tankeide|de|Saeki Tankeide|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tankeide|1=Saeki no Nifute|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828498}} (the father of Komaro), who, following {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}, executed {{ill|Prince Anahobe|de|Prinz Anahobe|ja|з©ҙз©ӮйғЁзҡҮеӯҗ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anahobe|qq=}}; {{ill|Saeki Ishiyu|de|Saeki Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ishiyu|1=Saeki no Iwayu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382879}}, the Ezo General after the conquest of Echigo; and {{ill|Saeki Katsuragi|de|Saeki Katsuragi|ja|дҪҗдјҜи‘ӣеҹҺ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Katsuragi|1=Saeki no Kazuragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382895}} (the grandson of Ishiyu), the Deputy General of the Eastern Expedition. Apart from Imao Hito, no one ascended to the position of {{ill|Giseikan|de|Giseikan|ja|иӯ°ж”ҝе®ҳ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giseikan|1=Giseikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632467}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}, and the clan eventually declined from being mid-level nobles to lower-Ranked officials ({{ill|Underground Officials|de|Underground Officials|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|lt=Underground Officials|lt_ja=ең°дёӢе®ҳдәә|lt_de=Underground Officials|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}). Until around the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku Period|1=Sengoku period}}, together with the {{ill|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|en|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Otomo Clan|1=ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)}}, {{ill|Wake Clan|de|Wake Clan|ja|е’Ңж°—ж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Clan|1=Wake clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417729}}, and the {{ill|Kudara no Konikishi clan|en|Kudara no Konikishi clan|WD=Q710197|lt=Baekje Royal Family|1=Kudara no Konikishi clan}}, the clan was considered one that received {{ill|Imperial, royal and noble ranks|en|Imperial, royal and noble ranks|WD=Q355567|lt=Noble Titles|1=Imperial, royal and noble ranks}} upon the accession of an emperor{{ill|Tajima no Kimoto|de|Tajima no Kimoto|ja|з”°еі¶е…¬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima no Kimoto|1=Isao Tajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45819717}} "The Establishment of Noble Titles вҖ“ Rituals, Service, and Conferment" in {{sfn|еҸІжһ—}} 71-1, {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988 AD|1=1988}}, p. 61. Among the lower-Ranked officials bearing the Saeki name, there is the {{ill|Ono Family|de|Ono-Familie|ja|е°ҸйҮҺе®¶ (дҪҗдјҜе§“)|lt=Ono Family|lt_ja=е°ҸйҮҺе®¶|lt_de=Ono-Familie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which produced the materia medica scholar {{ill|Ono Ranzan|en|Ono Ranzan|WD=Q11464264|lt=Ono Ransan|1=Ono Ranzan}}, and the painter {{ill|Kishi Ganku|en|Kishi Ganku|WD=Q555061|lt=Kishi Koma|1=Kishi Ganku}}, who founded the {{ill|Kishi School|de|Kishi-Schule|ja|еІёжҙҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi School|1=Kishi school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475951}} and served the {{ill|Arisugawa-no-miya|en|Arisugawa-no-miya|WD=Q2861427|lt=Arisugawa-no-miya|1=Arisugawa-no-miya}} family, bearing the Saeki name. == Related Clans == === Saeki Tsukuri === '''Saeki Tsukuri''' (-Miyatsuko) appears in the вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ under the {{ill|Ukyo|en|Ukyo|WD=Q2474484|lt=Right Capital|1=Ukyo}} Divine Separation section, and his former name was "Saeki Faction". According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he was a retainer of {{ill|Ichinobe no Oshiwa|en|Ichinobe no Oshiwa|WD=Q2297842|lt=Ichinobe Oshi|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}} and a descendant of {{ill|Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|de|Saeki Fraktion Naka-chiko|ja|дҪҗдјҜйғЁд»Іеӯҗ|lt=Naka-chiko|lt_ja=гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“|lt_de=Naka-chiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҪҗдјҜйғЁеЈІијӘ|1=Saekibe no Uruwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876437}}In the Pre-accession Annals of {{ill|Emperor YЕ«ryaku|en|Emperor YЕ«ryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor YЕ«ryaku|1=Emperor YЕ«ryaku}}, in the 3rd year, October 1 clause of {{ill|Emperor AnkЕҚ|en|Emperor AnkЕҚ|WD=Q361010|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor AnkЕҚ}}, he is referred to as "Saeki Faction Uruba" (вҖ“uruba), also known as "Naka-teko" (гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“). and, in the 5th year, February 5 clause of {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}}, he was appointed to oversee the Saeki factions from various provinces. === Saeki Obito === '''Saeki Obito''' (-Obito) appears in the вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} under the {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} category. == Saeki Faction == The Saeki Clan oversaw the central {{ill|Saeki people|en|Saeki people|WD=Q7398288|lt=Saeki Faction|1=Saeki people}} within the {{ill|Shinabe clans|en|Shinabe clans|WD=Q11418456|lt=Official Division|1=Shinabe clans}},{{Cite kotobank|Saeki Clan|encyclopedia=Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten 2nd edition|accessdate=2021-12-19}} while the regional Saeki Faction was overseen by {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki Nao|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Nao|1=Nao Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382874}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}. == [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] [[ja:дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ]] '''Saeki clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) refers to a group of families bearing the name "Saeki" as their {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji|1=Uji (clan)}} (clan name). __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == The Saeki clan traces its origins to {{ill|Amenootsuhiko|ja|еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenootsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939616|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|2=ja|3=гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|4=zh|5=еӨ©еҝҚж—Ҙе‘Ҫ}} (Ame no Oshi-hi no Mikoto), who guided {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|ja|еҪҰзҒ«з“ҠгҖ…жқөе°Ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} during the {{ill|Tenson kЕҚrin|en|Tenson kЕҚrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the heavenly grandson|1=Tenson kЕҚrin}}. The clan split from the {{ill|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|en|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=ЕҢtomo clan|1=ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)}} under the leadership of {{ill|ЕҢtomo no Muroya|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢtomo no Muroya|1=ЕҢtomo Muroya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432336}}, becoming a {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} (descendant of deities) lineage of {{ill|Tenshin|ja|еӨ©жҙҘзҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©жҙҘзҘһгғ»еӣҪжҙҘзҘһ|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442793}} origin. The clan name "Saeki" is believed to derive from their role in guarding the imperial court, as they were seen as those who "block (йҒ® sahe) external threats." This evolved into "Saeki" (гҒ•гҒҲгҒҚ) in modern phonetics, and in some cases as "Saiki" (гҒ•гӮҗгҒҚ). The Saeki clan served as {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|дјҙйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, leading the {{ill|Saeki people|en|Saeki people|WD=Q7398288|lt=Saeki-be|1=Saeki people}}, who were tasked with guarding the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|WD=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court of Japan|1=Royal court}}. The gates they protected were named "Saeki Gates," though during the Heian period, under {{ill|Tang culture|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang culture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е№іж–ҮеҢ–|1=TenpyЕҚ culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5525117}} influences, the name was changed to "Mokabe Gate" (и—»еЈҒй–Җ). Initially granted the {{ill|Kabane|ja|е§“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabane|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} of "Muraji" (йҖЈ), the Saeki clan, alongside the ЕҢtomo clan and others, was elevated to the Rank of "Sukune" (е®ҝзҰ°) during the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (685 CE). Prominent figures in the Saeki clan included {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|1=Saeki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382809}}, who assisted in the assassination of Soga no Iruka, and {{ill|Saeki no Imaemaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Imaemaro|1=Saeki no Imaemishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382787}}, a statesman who served as a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} and {{ill|Dazai no Sochi|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Sochi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§е®°еёҘ}}. Military leaders such as {{ill|Saeki no Tsurugite|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Tsurugite|1=Saeki no Nifute|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828498}}, who served under Soga no Umako, and {{ill|Saeki no Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ishiyu|1=Saeki no Iwayu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382879}}, a general during campaigns against the Emishi, were also notable. However, by the end of the Heian period, the clan gradually declined from mid-level aristocracy to {{ill|Chikage|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikage|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}} (bureaucrats of lower Rank). The Saeki name was carried on by families like the Ono family, who produced the herbalist {{ill|Ono Ranzan|en|Ono Ranzan|WD=Q11464264|lt=Ono Ranzan|1=Ono Ranzan}}, and artists such as {{ill|Kishi Ganku|en|Kishi Ganku|WD=Q555061|lt=Kishi Ganku|1=Kishi Ganku}} of the Kishi school. == Related Lineages == === Saeki no Miyatsuko (дҪҗдјҜйҖ ) The Saeki no Miyatsuko lineage is documented in the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}, appearing under the {{ill|Ukyo|en|Ukyo|WD=Q2474484|lt=Right Capital|1=Ukyo}} Shinbetsu. They were originally known as "Saeki-be" and managed the Saeki-be labor group. === Saeki no Obito (дҪҗдјҜйҰ–) The Saeki no Obito line appears in the Shinbetsu of {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} as recorded in the Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku. == Saeki-be == The Saeki-be, managed by the central Saeki clan, was a labor group organized as part of the {{ill|Shinabe clans|en|Shinabe clans|WD=Q11418456|lt=Shinbetsu|1=Shinabe clans}} system. Local Saeki-be groups were supervised by the {{ill|Saeki no Ason|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Ason|1=Nao Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382874}} lineage. == Tsukuri|de|Saeki Tsukuri|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tsukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Saeki Obito|de|Saeki Obito|ja|дҪҗдјҜйҰ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Obito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Saeki Clan''' (дҪҗдјҜж°Ҹ) is a {{ill|Uji (clan)|en|Uji (clan)|WD=Q2575910|lt=Uji|1=Uji (clan)}} whose name is "Saeki". __TOC__{{-}} == Overview == During the time of {{ill|Tenson kЕҚrin|en|Tenson kЕҚrin|WD=Q1084733|lt=Descent of the Heavenly Grandson|1=Tenson kЕҚrin}}, the ancestor, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q1056017|lt=Hikohirakininomikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, who was led by {{ill|Amenooshihi|de|Amenooshihi|ja|гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|lt=Heavenly Push Day Deity|lt_ja=еӨ©жҠјж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|lt_de=Amenooshihi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenooshihi|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939616|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|2=ja|3=гӮўгғЎгғҺгӮӘгӮ·гғ’|4=zh|5=еӨ©еҝҚж—Ҙе‘Ҫ}}, (Amenooshihi no Mikoto), is said to be the progenitor, and it is a {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} clan that split from the Otomo clan during the time of {{ill|Otomo no Sukune|de|Otomo no Sukune|ja|еӨ§дјҙе®ӨеұӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Sukune|1=ЕҢtomo Muroya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432336}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ {{ill|Sakyo|en|Sakyo|WD=Q11479660|lt=Sakyo|1=Sakyo}}, in the Divine Separation section.. After serving the royal authority and undertaking duties such as palace security, there is a theory that they came to be called "Saeki", a term meaning "those who block" (йҒ®гҒҺгӮӢиҖ…), referring to repelling external enemy attacks ({{ill|Historical kana orthography|en|Historical kana orthography|WD=Q1142552|lt=Historical Kana Usage|1=Historical kana orthography}} spells it "saheki", while {{ill|Modern kana usage|en|Modern kana usage|WD=Q2572881|lt=Modern Kana Usage|1=Modern kana usage}} spells it "saeki"). There is also a dialectal pronunciation "saiki" (also "sawiki"). As the central {{ill|BanzЕҚ|de|BanzЕҚ|ja|дјҙйҖ |12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/BanzЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Tomonomiyatsuko|WD=Q11381545}}, they led the {{ill|Saeki people|en|Saeki people|WD=Q7398288|lt=Saeki Faction|1=Saeki people}}, serving the {{ill|Imperial Court|de|Kaiserhof|ja|жңқе»· (ж—Ҙжң¬)|lt=Imperial Court|lt_ja=жңқе»·|lt_de=Kaiserhof|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Imperial Court of Japan|WD=Q30253177}} as palace gate guards and as a military force. Incidentally, the palace gate in charge of security was named {{ill|Saeki Gate|de|Saeki-Tor|ja|дҪҗдјҜй–Җ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Gate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to и—»еЈҒй–Җ|1=SЕҚhekimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17995614}} after the clan name, but at the {{ill|Heian Palace|en|Heian Palace|WD=Q304501|lt=Heian Palace|1=Heian Palace}}, under the influence of {{ill|Tang-style Culture|de|Tang-Kultur|ja|е”җйўЁж–ҮеҢ–|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tang-style Culture|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е№іж–ҮеҢ–|1=TenpyЕҚ culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5525117}}, it was renamed to {{ill|Moss Wall Gate|de|Mooswandtor|ja|и—»еЈҒй–Җ|lt=Moss Wall Gate (SЕҚheki Gate)|lt_ja=и—»еЈҒпјҲгҒқгҒҶгҒёгҒҚпјүй–Җ|lt_de=Mooswandtor|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moss Wall Gate|1=SЕҚhekimon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17995614}}вҖҳ{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiriyaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}вҖҷ {{ill|KЕҚnin (era)|en|KЕҚnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=KЕҚnin|1=KЕҚnin (era)}} 9th year ({{ill|818|en|818|WD=Q25083|lt=818 AD|1=818}}) April 27 clause.. The {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} was originally {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}, but in the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|WD=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685 AD|1=685}}), together with members of the same group such as the {{ill|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|en|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Otomo Clan|1=ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)}}, they were granted the surname {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukunabe|1=Sukune}}. In the {{ill|Main Palace Office|de|Hauptpalastamt|ja|дё»ж®ҝеҜ®|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace Office|1=ShudenryЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368767}}, the name of the Saeki faction remains as one of the noble divisions. Notable figures include {{ill|Saeki Komaro|de|Saeki Komaro|ja|дҪҗдјҜеӯҗйә»е‘Ӯ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Komaro|1=Saeki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382809}}, who, following {{ill|Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|de|Prinz Nakano-ЕҢe|ja|дёӯеӨ§е…„зҡҮеӯҗ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano-ЕҢe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©жҷәеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q314802}}, assassinated {{ill|Soga no Iruka|en|Soga no Iruka|WD=Q736621|lt=Soga no Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}}, and {{ill|Saeki Imao Hito|de|Saeki Imao Hito|ja|дҪҗдјҜд»ҠжҜӣдәә|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Imao Hito|1=Saeki no Imaemishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382787}}, who was active in government from the Nara to the Heian period and held positions such as {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}} and {{ill|Dazai no Mikoto|de|Dazai no Mikoto|ja|еӨӘе®°еёҘ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§е®°еёҘ|1=Dazai no Sochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434484}}. The clan also produced many warriors, including {{ill|Saeki Tankeide|de|Saeki Tankeide|ja|дҪҗдјҜдё№зөҢжүӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Tankeide|1=Saeki no Nifute|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45828498}} (the father of Komaro), who, following {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga no Umako|1=Soga no Umako}}, executed {{ill|Prince Anahobe|de|Prinz Anahobe|ja|з©ҙз©ӮйғЁзҡҮеӯҗ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Anahobe|qq=}}; {{ill|Saeki Ishiyu|de|Saeki Ishiyu|ja|дҪҗдјҜзҹіж№Ҝ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ishiyu|1=Saeki no Iwayu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382879}}, the Ezo General after the conquest of Echigo; and {{ill|Saeki Katsuragi|de|Saeki Katsuragi|ja|дҪҗдјҜи‘ӣеҹҺ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Katsuragi|1=Saeki no Kazuragi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382895}} (the grandson of Ishiyu), the Deputy General of the Eastern Expedition. Apart from Imao Hito, no one ascended to the position of {{ill|Giseikan|de|Giseikan|ja|иӯ°ж”ҝе®ҳ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Giseikan|1=Giseikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632467}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}, and the clan eventually declined from being mid-level nobles to lower-Ranked officials ({{ill|Underground Officials|de|Underground Officials|ja|ең°дёӢе®¶|1=Jigeke|2=ja|3=ең°дёӢе®¶|lt=Jigeke|lt=Underground Officials|lt_ja=ең°дёӢе®ҳдәә|lt_de=Underground Officials|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Underground Officials|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigeke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11424052}}). Until around the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku Period|1=Sengoku period}}, together with the {{ill|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|en|ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Otomo Clan|1=ЕҢtomo clan (ancient)}}, {{ill|Wake Clan|de|Wake Clan|ja|е’Ңж°—ж°Ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake Clan|1=Wake clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11417729}}, and the {{ill|Kudara no Konikishi clan|en|Kudara no Konikishi clan|WD=Q710197|lt=Baekje Royal Family|1=Kudara no Konikishi clan}}, the clan was considered one that received {{ill|Imperial, royal and noble ranks|en|Imperial, royal and noble ranks|WD=Q355567|lt=Noble Titles|1=Imperial, royal and noble ranks}} upon the accession of an emperor{{ill|Tajima no Kimoto|de|Tajima no Kimoto|ja|з”°еі¶е…¬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajima no Kimoto|1=Isao Tajima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45819717}} "The Establishment of Noble Titles вҖ“ Rituals, Service, and Conferment" in {{sfn|еҸІжһ—}} 71-1, {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988 AD|1=1988}}, p. 61. Among the lower-Ranked officials bearing the Saeki name, there is the {{ill|Ono Family|de|Ono-Familie|ja|е°ҸйҮҺе®¶ (дҪҗдјҜе§“)|lt=Ono Family|lt_ja=е°ҸйҮҺе®¶|lt_de=Ono-Familie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono Family|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which produced the materia medica scholar {{ill|Ono Ranzan|en|Ono Ranzan|WD=Q11464264|lt=Ono Ransan|1=Ono Ranzan}}, and the painter {{ill|Kishi Ganku|en|Kishi Ganku|WD=Q555061|lt=Kishi Koma|1=Kishi Ganku}}, who founded the {{ill|Kishi School|de|Kishi-Schule|ja|еІёжҙҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi School|1=Kishi school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475951}} and served the {{ill|Arisugawa-no-miya|en|Arisugawa-no-miya|WD=Q2861427|lt=Arisugawa-no-miya|1=Arisugawa-no-miya}} family, bearing the Saeki name. == Related Clans == === Saeki Tsukuri === '''Saeki Tsukuri''' (-Miyatsuko) appears in the вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ under the {{ill|Ukyo|en|Ukyo|WD=Q2474484|lt=Right Capital|1=Ukyo}} Divine Separation section, and his former name was "Saeki Faction". According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he was a retainer of {{ill|Ichinobe no Oshiwa|en|Ichinobe no Oshiwa|WD=Q2297842|lt=Ichinobe Oshi|1=Ichinobe no Oshiwa}} and a descendant of {{ill|Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|de|Saeki Fraktion Naka-chiko|ja|дҪҗдјҜйғЁд»Іеӯҗ|lt=Naka-chiko|lt_ja=гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“|lt_de=Naka-chiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Faction Naka-chiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to дҪҗдјҜйғЁеЈІијӘ|1=Saekibe no Uruwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876437}}In the Pre-accession Annals of {{ill|Emperor YЕ«ryaku|en|Emperor YЕ«ryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor YЕ«ryaku|1=Emperor YЕ«ryaku}}, in the 3rd year, October 1 clause of {{ill|Emperor AnkЕҚ|en|Emperor AnkЕҚ|WD=Q361010|lt=Emperor Ankan|1=Emperor AnkЕҚ}}, he is referred to as "Saeki Faction Uruba" (вҖ“uruba), also known as "Naka-teko" (гҒӘгҒӢгҒЎгҒ“). and, in the 5th year, February 5 clause of {{ill|Emperor Ninken|en|Emperor Ninken|WD=Q357266|lt=Emperor Ninken|1=Emperor Ninken}}, he was appointed to oversee the Saeki factions from various provinces. === Saeki Obito === '''Saeki Obito''' (-Obito) appears in the вҖҳ{{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|en|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=New Compilation of Surnames|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}вҖҷ in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|WD=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} under the {{ill|Divine Separation|de|Divine Separation|ja|зҘһеҲҘ|1=Shinbetsu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Separation|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}} category. == Saeki Faction == The Saeki Clan oversaw the central {{ill|Saeki people|en|Saeki people|WD=Q7398288|lt=Saeki Faction|1=Saeki people}} within the {{ill|Shinabe clans|en|Shinabe clans|WD=Q11418456|lt=Official Division|1=Shinabe clans}},{{Cite kotobank|Saeki Clan|encyclopedia=Sekai Dai Hyakka Jiten 2nd edition|accessdate=2021-12-19}} while the regional Saeki Faction was overseen by {{ill|Saeki Nao|de|Saeki Nao|ja|дҪҗдјҜзӣҙ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Nao|1=Nao Saeki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382874}} {{sfn|ж—Ҙжң¬еӨ§зҷҫз§‘е…Ёжӣё}}. ==
